CheckTitle: Review admin-consented delegated permissions with broad scope
Description: Identifies OAuth2 delegated permission grants (oauth2PermissionGrants) where admin consent has been given for all users (consentType=AllPrincipals) with scopes that include high-risk permissions like Mail.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All, or Directory.ReadWrite.All.
Risk: Admin-consented delegated permissions apply to all users in the tenant. If a third-party app has Mail.ReadWrite consented for all users, access to every user mailbox via that app, with attacker needing only a valid user token. This is the delegated equivalent of over-privileged application permissions.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review all admin-consented delegated permissions. Revoke grants that are no longer needed. Prefer user-consented grants scoped to individual users where possible. Use application access policies for mail scoping.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark

SourceNotes: Framework control IDs were AI-generated. Excessive delegated permission review is a well-known Entra ID security best practice.
